module_map.responsibility: Token definitions and TokenType enum for lexical analysis output
Lexical scanner: converts SQL string to Token list via character-by-character scanning
Syntax parser: consumes Token list and builds AST via recursive descent parsing; maintains function registry via FUNCTIONS dict mapping SQL names to Expression constructors
Core AST node hierarchy; all SQL constructs as Expression subclasses (string.py, temporal.py, query.py, etc.); FUNCTION_BY_NAME registry auto-generated from Func subclasses
AST→SQL: converts Expression tree back to SQL string; dialect-aware via dispatch methods for each Expression type
Dialect-specific parsers/generators: extends Parser and Generator for SQL dialect variants (BigQuery, Snowflake, Spark, etc.); overrides FUNCTIONS, token handling, and generation methods
Query optimization passes (simplify, qualify columns, pushdown predicates, etc.); transforms AST to equivalent optimized form
Query execution engine: evaluates optimized AST against data; Python-based runtime for DML/SELECT

types_produced: Token (with TokenType, value, line, col, comments, etc.)
pipeline_trace.flow: SQL string scanned character-by-character; pattern matching identifies keywords, literals, operators; emits Token stream

pipeline_trace.key_function: FUNCTION_BY_NAME = {name: func for func in ALL_FUNCTIONS for name in func.sql_names()}
pipeline_trace.flow: Parser.FUNCTIONS merges FUNCTION_BY_NAME (Expression constructors) with custom builders (build_hex, build_logarithm, etc.); maps SQL function names to callable factories
Recursive descent parser consumes token stream; builds AST via exp.* constructors; FUNCTIONS dict called to construct Func nodes for recognized functions

pipeline_trace.key_function: optimizer.optimize(ast: Expression, read: Dialect, write: Dialect, ...) -> Expression
pipeline_trace.flow: Optimizer passes (annotate_types, qualify_columns, simplify, pushdown_predicates, etc.) transform AST in-place or return new tree; maintains semantic equivalence
Generator traverses AST; dispatches on expression.__class__.__name__ to find generator method (e.g., generator_levenshtein); concatenates SQL fragments; respects dialect-specific SQL syntax

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central AST node hierarchy. Parser, Generator, Optimizer, Dialects, Executor all depend on exp.* classes. Every SQL construct is an Expression. FUNCTION_BY_NAME registry (built via subclasses inspection) connects Expression to Parser. Expressions inherit from core classes (Expression, Func, Binary, etc.).

Converts Expression trees to SQL strings. Imported by Parser (for error generation), Dialects (subclass for dialect-specific output), __init__ (transpile API), Transforms, Optimizer (debugging), Executor (query execution). Contains Generator base class and unsupported_args decorator used across dialects.

Entry point for SQL text→AST. Imported by Dialects (subclass for dialect-specific parsing), __init__ (parse API), Schema (reference only). FUNCTIONS dict bridges Expression constructors and Token stream. Uses tokens.TokenType, trie.TrieResult for keyword lookup, helper utilities.

pattern_for_new_functions.step_1_expression_definition: Add class in expressions/string.py (or appropriate submodule). Inherit from Expression and Func. Define arg_types dict mapping parameter names to (required, optional) bool. Set _sql_names list if aliases needed.
pattern_for_new_functions.step_2_parser_registration: Expression class auto-registered via FUNCTION_BY_NAME (no parser change needed unless custom builder required). If builder needed (e.g., multi-arg handling), add to Parser.FUNCTIONS dict in parser.py around line 289-382.
pattern_for_new_functions.step_3_generator_dispatch: Generator.generate() dispatches on expression.__class__.__name__. Add method generator_myfunction(self, expression) returning SQL string. Located in generator.py. Dialects subclass and override if needed.
pattern_for_new_functions.step_4_dialect_overrides: For dialect-specific SQL syntax, subclass Generator in dialects/dialect_name.py; override generator_myfunction() method.

risks_for_new_functions: Missing generator method → generate() will fail for function nodes (falls back to default Expression rendering)
Dialect mismatch → target SQL may not support function; override in dialects/ required
Type checking → Optimizer.annotate_types may not infer correct return type; add to optimizer/annotate_types.py if needed
Tokenizer → Dialect.tokenizer may need keyword registration if not auto-detected (e.g., new reserved words)

tool_usage.params: 10 calls: locate files (ls -la), search imports (rg '^from|^import'), find FUNCTIONS dict line numbers (grep -n), search for specific functions (rg 'class.*Levenshtein')
5 calls: parser.py FUNCTIONS dict (lines 289-382), expressions/string.py Levenshtein class (lines 74-82), __init__.py pipeline entry points (lines 73-177), generator.py imports (lines 1-50)

summary.pipeline_architecture: Strictly layered: Tokenizer (tokens.py → List[Token]) → Parser (parser.py uses FUNCTION_BY_NAME from expressions/) → Expression AST → Optimizer (optional, in-place transforms) → Generator (generator.py, dialect-aware dispatch) → SQL string. Dialects subclass Parser/Generator; executor/optimizer/schema provide utilities.
summary.hub_pattern: Three hubs (expressions, generator, parser) form a triangle: expressions are nodes, generator renders to SQL, parser consumes tokens. All other modules (dialects, optimizer, executor, schema) converge here. expressions is most connected (central node).
summary.function_addition_pattern: Define Expression subclass with arg_types → auto-registered in FUNCTION_BY_NAME via introspection → parser picks up via FUNCTIONS dict → generator must have corresponding method. Levenshtein already follows this pattern perfectly.
summary.analysis_confidence: High. Used only native tools (Bash, Read, Grep). Verified key files and code patterns. No private tools or environment assumptions.
